feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Install on-premise partner add-ons for S/4HANA 2023 private cloud
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

We had released add-ons(partner) for our customers and they are on-premise. 

Is there any difference in installing the partner add-ons on S/4HANA private cloud or its same as like for on-premise? 

What is the procedure to release private cloud compatible add-ons and customers to install them on private cloud?

Thanks,

Prabhakaran

entry.title: partition (1) contains *** records. A table partition cannot contain more than 2,147,483,64
entry.content:
The Table S*** has 2 partitions as we are reaching 2Billion mark, but we are observing that the Partition-1 of the the table is still increasing by few entries everyday. it is very close to 2Billion records and we would need suggestion on the house keeping for this table to keep the entries under the limit.Please find the screenshot of Historical Growth based on Daily Data: 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Create item level with the logic
entry.content:
I have SellingPrice and BuyingPrice in both Header level and Item level. And has a "Copy" button in Header level. 

What I want to do is when user click the Copy button, the value of SellingPrice and BuyingPrice in header level will be assigned to item level and one record is created. 

Everytime user click the Copy button, item level should be created with the value of Header level data.

Below is the logic I have written in Copy action. But it gets error of "Last runtime error at: 04/28/2025, 04:51:37, error text: The key of business object YY1_PRICINGINFORMATION is wrongly specified." 

Although the YY1_PRICINGINFORMATION is correct, it gives the same error. The key has value "0000000001"

* Step 1: Get Root Entity Key
*DATA(lv_key_cleaned) = PRICINGINFORMATION-PriceID. 
DATA(lv_key) = |{ PRICINGINFORMATION-PriceID }|.

* Step 2: Get the root node object
DATA(lo_root) = write->get_root(
business_object_id = 'YY1_PRICINGINFORMATION' 
key = 'lv_key'
).

* Step 3: Prepare data for child node (ITEM)
DATA(ls_item) = VALUE YY1_HISTORY_PRICINGINFORMATION( 
BuyingPrice = PRICINGINFORMATION-Price
SellingPrice = PRICINGINFORMATION-SellingPrice
).

* Step 4: Create child under root
lo_root->create_child(
EXPORTING
node_id = 'History' 
data = ls_item
).

* Step 5: Return success message
message = VALUE #( severity = 'S' text = 'Item created from Action.' ).

 

Could you pls guide us how to change my logic to fulfil requirement? If that way is not okay, could you pls guide me the possible way? 

Thanks in advance. 

Best Regards,

Pyae

entry.title: TM New Attribute to addition selection profile and use it in Planning Cockpit
entry.content:
This blog post explains how to add a custom attribute to the Additional Selection Profile for Freight Units (FU) and Freight Orders (FO), and how to use this custom field in the planning cockpit.


The requirement was to filter the Transportation Cockpit based on a custom field. Since it was not possible to add the custom field directly, an enhancement was implemented to make the custom field available in the Additional Selection Profile. Once added, this custom field can be used to filter results within the Transportation Cockpit.

Step 1: 

Add the custom field ZZVEHICLE_TYPE to the structure /SCMTMS/D_TORROT, and ensure that the save strategy is implemented to populate ZZVEHICLE_TYPE with the required value.

Step 2:

Enhance the structure /SCMTMS/S_TOR_QUERY, which is used in /SCMTMS/S_TOR_Q_FU_STG_PLN, and add the custom field ZZVEHICLE_TYPE

Adding the field ZVEHICLE_TYPE to the above structure will make it available as an attribute in the Additional Selection Profile.

Step 3:

Additionally, implement the method GET_SHLP_FIELD_WHITELIST of class /SCMTMS/CL_PROF_HELPER to allow the custom field to be visible in the Additional Selection Attributes.

Step 4:

Now, the field ZZVEHICLE_TYPE is visible and has been added to the Additional Selection Attributes.

Step 5:

Enhance the method GET_QUERY_ENHANCE_TABLE of class /SCMTMS/CL_TOR_Q_SUPERCLASS to specify the TM database table that should be referenced for the custom Z field added in the selection profile and used for filtering.

After completing the above developments and implementations, the Transportation Cockpit will be able to filter based on the new additional fields in the selection profile.

 

 

I hope this blog post will be helpful to SAP developers.


Regards,
Prince Agarwal

entry.title: S/4 HANA migration : Factors leading to success from a customer perspective
entry.content:
“How We Nailed Our S/4HANA Migration — 4 Hours Ahead of Schedule during Go-live weekend”

As SAP gradually phases out support for ECC by the end of 2027, a vast number of companies are making the move to S/4HANA. In line with this trend, my organization in the utilities sector initiated our migration project in 2023. I’m happy to share that we recently completed a successful go-live — four hours ahead of schedule!

The purpose of this article is to share the key factors that contributed to our success, with the hope that they will support others on the same journey. I am a SAP Certified Associate: SAP Activate Project Manager with experience across various SAP projects. Our migration was predominantly Brownfield, involving a transition from an on-premises setup to a private cloud environment.

Below are the key criteria that made our migration successful:

1. Preparation

SAP provides a comprehensive Readiness Check package free of charge to all migration customers. One of the essential components is the Simplification Item List, which is executed in your SAP ERP system in preparation for the conversion to S/4HANA.[1] This analysis generates a list of:

  • New functionalities available in S/4HANA
  • Deprecated functionalities with recommended alternatives

This list helps the project team assess the impact on each SAP module, enabling more accurate resource allocation and project planning.

Another important preparatory step is addressing knowledge gaps within the team. A Learning Needs Analysis [2] can identify which team members require upskilling to ensure a smooth migration.

2. Design

During the Explore phase of SAP’s Activate methodology, [3] fit-to-standard workshops are conducted. These sessions compare the current (as-is) processes with future (to-be) scenarios and identify any gaps — referred to as Delta Requirements. [4] The project team must then determine whether these gaps can be addressed through standard SAP functionality or require enhancements.

Phases of SAP Activate[5]

Crucially, these workshops are guided by the Simplification Item List, which may include items needing key business decisions. For instance, when a functionality change offers multiple solution paths, the input of business stakeholders — key users and process owners — is essential. Their participation in these workshops ensures the resulting functional design meets business needs.

3. Test Strategy

A solid test strategy, developed during the Explore phase, is essential. Various types of testing occur during the SAP implementation:

  • Unit Testing: Conducted by the implementation partner to verify individual transactions.
  • System Integration Testing: The customer’s responsibility; ensures module dependencies and integrations (SAP and non-SAP) work as expected and data integrity is preserved.
  • User Acceptance Testing (UAT): Validates whether the system supports day-to-day business operations.[6]

Additionally, consider performance and penetration testing, especially for mission-critical systems.

4. Data Validation Strategy

Ensuring the accuracy and completeness of migrated data is crucial — not only for business continuity but also for IT audit compliance. Collaborate with your IT auditor early to identify critical data points, and validate these through comparison of pre- and post-migration datasets in each cycle (SBX, DEV, QAS, PROD). A thorough validation process enables the IT auditor to approve the decommissioning of ECC systems confidently.

5. Benefits of carrying out Dry Run

Before the go-live weekend, a Dry Run (dress rehearsal) allows the team to test the full cutover plan in a controlled environment. Performing this from ECC Production to S/4HANA Production reveals any environment-specific issues in advance and provides confidence in your cutover timeline and activities. This proactive step greatly contributes to a smooth go-live.

6. Predefined Acceptance Criteria

Testing often reveals hundreds of issues. Without predefined acceptance criteria, it becomes difficult for project management to make informed go/no-go decisions. One recommended approach is the Severity Classification Method, which categorizes defects as follows:

  • Showstopper: The process cannot proceed
  • Workaround: The process can proceed with a workaround
  • No Impact: No effect on processes

By classifying all issues, the project team can focus on resolving showstoppers within the test timeline. Tools like SAP Cloud ALM offer efficient defect tracking and allow configuration of severity levels to streamline defect management.

Final Thoughts

Migrating to S/4HANA is a complex endeavor, but with thorough preparation, collaborative design, rigorous testing, and a proactive mindset, it is entirely achievable. I hope these lessons offer valuable guidance to others preparing for or undertaking their own migrations.

 

 

[1] SAP S/4HANA Simplification Item Check — How to do … — SAP Community

[2] Planning a Learning Needs Analysis

[3] Analyzing each phase of SAP Activate

[4] Conducting Design Workshops

[5] Analyzing each phase of SAP Activate

[6] Explaining the Test Strategy

entry.title: Is SAP Stock Room Management compatible with Handling Unit Management and Serial Number Management?
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am currently working on a project involving SAP Stock Room Management in S/4HANA. I would like to confirm whether Stock Room Management is fully compatible with Handling Unit Management (HUM),  Serial Number Management functionalities and Fiori applications. 

From the SAP documentation, it is not fully clear whether it's compatible or not.

Has anyone successfully implemented HUM and Serial Number Management with Stock Room Management?

Any official SAP reference or clarification would be highly appreciated!

Thank you in advance for your support!

entry.title: CDS View for Equipment Partner Functions, A_EquipmentPartner
entry.content:
Hi everone,

I am looking for a CDS View containing Equipment / Technical Object Partner information, like A_EquipmentPartner or displayed in https://myxxx.s4hana.cloud.sap/ui#MaintenanceObject-display?sap-ui-tech-hint=WDA

This crutial to report equipments by customer and thus sales representative etc.

Thank you and best regards
Tim

entry.title: How to authorize M_MSEG_LGO without implicitely authorizing ALL storage locations of the division
entry.content:
Dear authorization gurus,

As we know we can leverage authorization object M_MSEG_LGO to grant access to specific storage locations (LGORT) which have been declared in the customizing in a way that these are to be controlled at movement type level.

We can find which storage locations are controlled by object M_MSEG_LGO by looking at column XBLGO in table T0001L

Now if I take, for instance, MIGO I can see the transaction program's performs a check on a storage location to find out if it's to be controlled at movement level or not. And depending on how it's to be controlled will trigger different authorization checks scenarii.

 

Case 1) storage location is not to be controled at movement level (no XBLGO flag)
Objects which are checked are :

M_MSEG_WMB (ACTVT + WERKS)
M_MSEG_BMB (ACTVT + BWART)

Case 2) storage location is to be controled at movement level (XBLGO flag is set)
Objects which are checked are :

M_MSEG_WMB (ACTVT + WERKS)
M_MSEG_BMB (ACTVT + BWART)
+ M_MSEG_LGO (ACTVT + WERKS + LGORT + BWART)

What we see here is that for storage locations not part of the controlled ones, only WERKS & movement types (BWART) are actually checked. Basically, you're allowed at division level, no matter wich storage location.

What we also see here, in case 2, is that for storage location which are to be controlled, we nevertheless control redondant objects M_MSEG_WMB & M_MSEG_BMB, meaning that to actually access a controled storage location you ALSO have to authorize the whole division's through objects _WMB & _BMB.

Wouldn't it be better if we had :

Case 1)

M_MSEG_WMB (ACTVT + WERKS)
M_MSEG_BMB (ACTVT + BWART)

Case 2)

Only M_MSEG_LGO (ACTVT + WERKS + LGORT + BWART)

This would allow real sniper-like control over specific storage locations (which is the classical usecase). 
I just can't figure a usecase where I woud like to allow first ALL division's storage locations in order to allow only one of these. Sounds like a design flaw to me.

I see program LMIGOKA1 was updated with a note to fix a lazy control for display rights, but I wonder if this did not, in the end, broke some logic here. We could somehow patch the code and remove the checks on WMB & BMB for XBLGO storage locations... wouldn't we ?

The only standard solution I see right now would require to create a new WERKS to isolate my special storage locations or to remove check from SU24 of MIGO for these objects (with potentially biblical proportion problems ahead...)

Anyone has any experience with this already, any smarter approach ?

entry.title: Do i need to create an index CDHDR?
entry.content:
Hi,
I am querying CDHDR table with below fields.

OBJECTCLAS
OBJECTID
UDATE

I see some 4 indexes already created with one Z1(say) Index created with below 2 fields

OBJECTCLAS
UDATE

which means i am using extra field in my above query:

Is it a good idea to create another Index with extra field(like below) for my requirement?

OBJECTCLAS
OBJECTID
UDATE
Pls suggest.

entry.title: Request Application Job Template for Custom Analytical Query Execution
entry.content:
As my client has requirement to schedule a job to execute Custom Analytical Query every month, do you have any suggestion for me?
as I cannot find any template related to Custom Analytical Query now.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Allocation of landed costs to purchase items
entry.content:
Hello community,

please help me to implement the following scenario in SAP S/4HANA cloud, public eddition:

1. I have an invoiced purchase order with two materials;

2. Only after the purchase order in step 1 completed, I have an additional purchase document for landed costs such as: delivery costs, customs and so on. (Supliers of those two documents are of course different.)

3. That additional costs from step 2 needs be allocated to purchase items (of step 1) so, that the moving average cost per item is increased. 

Could you please provide a proper solution for this scenario?

entry.title: Mass Changes to Purchasing Info Records Fiori app _On-Premise SAP Version.
entry.content:
Hi Team,

 

We want to activate Mass Changes to Purchasing Info Records fiori app to create/update/delete multiple purchase info records.

 

As per below mentioned blog, this functionality is available in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

 

Mass Changes to Purchasing Info Records: Offline Mode | SAP Help Portal

 

Can you please let us know how we can activate same functionality in current On-Premise SAP Version.

 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: MRP live considering takt time
entry.content:
Specifically, this concerns the takt time including the lot-sizing procedure (it's in the MRP1 view of the material master data). Currently, the MRP Live doesn't support the takt time, and you're forced to use MRP Classic for the corresponding materials.

Is there any plan from SAP about whether and when the takt time might also be available with MRP Live, or will be disabled as functionality because of it’s based on old techniques and not in scope or part of SAP’ s roadmap?

Many thanks’ in advance for your support, I’m looking forward to  any feedback.

entry.title: SUM Shadow inctance on a remote application server
entry.content: Is it possible to run a shadow instance on a remote application server? The parameters for the shadow instance only allow to enter a system number, not the server names!

entry.title: One SF EC Instance mapped to two different S4 Hana system
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Our customer want;s to replication mapping with one SF instance with two different S4 HANA systems

entry.title: Error in establishing IDoc connection between SAP CPI and S/4HANA
entry.content:
We are currently trying to establish a connection between SAP CPI and S/4HANA using the IDoc adapter. Our system currently only uses OData, and we wanted to explore more options in connectivity.

To start our project, we are trying to send a sales order from SAP CPI to S4. The IDoc is ORDERS05, in XML format and the structure is correct. See below:

<ORDERS05>
	<IDOC BEGIN="1">
		<EDI_DC40 SEGMENT="1">
			<TABNAM>EDI_DC40</TABNAM>
			<MANDT>120</MANDT>
			<DOCNUM></DOCNUM>
			<DOCREL>755</DOCREL>
			<STATUS>53</STATUS>
			<DIRECT>2</DIRECT>
			<OUTMOD/>
			<IDOCTYP>ORDERS05</IDOCTYP>
			<MESTYP>ORDERS</MESTYP>
			<SNDPOR>CPIDEV</SNDPOR>
			<SNDPRT>KU</SNDPRT>
			<SNDPRN>C000003</SNDPRN>
			<RCVPOR>SAPDS4</RCVPOR>
			<RCVPRT>KU</RCVPRT>
			<RCVPRN>C000003</RCVPRN>
			<CREDAT>20250402</CREDAT>
			<CRETIM>090303</CRETIM>
		</EDI_DC40>
		<E1EDK01>
			<ACTION>00</ACTION>
			<BSART>DS</BSART>
			<BELNR>30114186</BELNR>
		</E1EDK01>
		<E1EDK14>
			<QUALF>019</QUALF>
			<ORGID>ZEDI</ORGID>
		</E1EDK14>
		<E1EDK03>
			<IDDAT>010</IDDAT>
			<DATUM>20250402</DATUM>
		</E1EDK03>
		<E1EDK03>
			<IDDAT>002</IDDAT>
			<DATUM>20250403</DATUM>
		</E1EDK03>
		<E1EDKA1>
			<PARVW>WE</PARVW>
			<NAME1>MXXX KXXX</NAME1>
			<STRAS>000 RXXXX</STRAS>
			<ORT01>FXXXXX LXXXX</ORT01>
			<PSTLZ>00000</PSTLZ>
			<TELF1>000-000-0000</TELF1>
			<REGIO>NJ</REGIO>
		</E1EDKA1>
		<E1EDKA1>
			<PARVW>AG</PARVW>
			<LIFNR>93906NPAW</LIFNR>
		</E1EDKA1>
		<E1EDK02>
			<QUALF>001</QUALF>
			<BELNR>30114186</BELNR>
			<DATUM>20250402</DATUM>
		</E1EDK02>
		<E1EDK17>
			<QUALF>Z01</QUALF>
			<LKOND>CG</LKOND>
		</E1EDK17>
		<E1EDKT1>
			<TDID>ZEDH</TDID>
			<E1EDKT2>
				<TDLINE>IA|93906</TDLINE>
				<TDFORMAT>/</TDFORMAT>
			</E1EDKT2>
			<E1EDKT2>
				<TDLINE>SL|CG</TDLINE>
				<TDFORMAT>/</TDFORMAT>
			</E1EDKT2>
			<E1EDKT2>
				<TDLINE>VN|93906</TDLINE>
				<TDFORMAT>/</TDFORMAT>
			</E1EDKT2>
			<E1EDKT2>
				<TDLINE>ST|NPAW</TDLINE>
				<TDFORMAT>/</TDFORMAT>
			</E1EDKT2>
			<E1EDKT2>
				<TDLINE>BT|NPAW</TDLINE>
				<TDFORMAT>/</TDFORMAT>
			</E1EDKT2>
		</E1EDKT1>
		<E1EDKT1>
			<TDID>ZSCH</TDID>
			<E1EDKT2>
				<TDLINE>:C000::003</TDLINE>
				<TDFORMAT>/</TDFORMAT>
			</E1EDKT2>
		</E1EDKT1>
		<E1EDP01>
			<POSEX>1</POSEX>
			<MENGE>2</MENGE>
			<MENEE>EA</MENEE>
			<VPREI>112.2</VPREI>
			<E1EDP19>
				<QUALF>003</QUALF>
				<IDTNR>806134977252</IDTNR>
			</E1EDP19>
			<E1EDP19>
				<QUALF>001</QUALF>
				<IDTNR>4645188</IDTNR>
			</E1EDP19>
			<E1EDP19>
				<QUALF>002</QUALF>
				<IDTNR>H981101-AGB</IDTNR>
			</E1EDP19>
		</E1EDP01>
		<E1EDS01>
			<SUMID>001</SUMID>
			<SUMME>1</SUMME>
			<SUNIT>EA</SUNIT>
		</E1EDS01>
	</IDOC>
</ORDERS05>

Before trying to send this file, we have done the basics such as activate IDoc via SICF, creating an RFC connection in SM59, registering the IDoc service in SRTIDoc, we added the IDoc URL resource path (sap/bc/srt/idoc) in SAP Cloud Connector. We also created a new port via WE21. We followed this blog: SAP CPI to SAP ERP and SAP ERP to SAP CPI and did every step.

However, when we tried to send the file, we got this error in SAP CPI Monitoring:

I checked error logs via SRT_ELOG in S4, and got the same error:

We cannot find any blog or SAP note that could help us. Is there anyone who experienced this and how were you able to fix it?

Thank you very much!

entry.title: API_SERVICE_ORDER_SRV is not holding value in RefServiceOrderTemplate when triggered from C4C
entry.content:
Requirement: In C4C Ticket application when we click on ‘Create Service’ order (follow-up document) button, expectation is to Pass Service order Template ID from C4C to S4 Hana through Service order creation API (API_SERVICE_ORDER_SRV), In S4 Hana, while system creating Service order copy all the Items maintained on Service order Template into Service order.

But, API_SERVICE_ORDER_SRV is not holding value in RefServiceOrderTemplate when triggered from C4C into S4 Hana.

entry.title: Activating Audit trail log for Accounting tables & specific fields
entry.content:
How to activate/check Audit trail log for Accounting tables (BKPF/BSEG/etc.) & specific fields for entries modified directly at DB table level?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: SAP PPDS Release move the scheduling status to deallocated
entry.content:
Hello! My process is:

1. create planned order ppds

2. schedule and fix dates for planned order in PPDS

3. convert planned order to produzione order in ppds

4. release the productioon order in S4.

Untill step 3 the scheduling status remains allocated (dispatched).

after release, in s4, the production order scheduling status becomes deallocated in ppds.

is it normal behaviour? should i open a SAP note?

someone had the same problem?

Luca Pastura

entry.title: How to debug data migrations in the Migration Cockpit?
entry.content:
Dear SAP,

We have created a custom Migration Object for usage in the Migration Cockpit (based on the object Supplier), and its simulations were successful. When we tried to migrate it though, some unexpected errors were thrown, and we'd like to debug such migrations in the future to avoid similar issues. We checked the KBAs 3047880 and 3059453, but they only mention debugging data simulations.

Considering this, could you inform us:

  • How to debug migrations (instead of simulations) performed using the Migration Cockpit?

Our system is an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. Thanks!

Best regards,
Data migration team


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.
